question.title: ao-kernel OTEL native — OpenTelemetry instrumentation
question.body:
ao-kernel'e OTEL (OpenTelemetry) native instrumentation ekliyoruz. Mevcut durum: streaming (experimental), MCP server (4 tool), 126 test.

Neyi instrument etmeliyiz?

Potansiyel spanlar:
1. llm_call — her LLM cagrisinda (provider, model, latency, tokens, status)
2. stream_call — streaming LLM cagrisinda (first_token_ms, chunk_count)
3. policy_check — policy evaluation (policy_ref, decision, violations)
4. quality_gate — output quality check (gate_id, passed/failed)
5. mcp_tool_call — MCP tool invocation (tool_name, decision)

Potansiyel metrikler:
1. ao.llm.call.duration — histogram (provider, model, status)
2. ao.llm.tokens.usage — counter (provider, input/output)
3. ao.policy.check.total — counter (policy, decision)
4. ao.quality.gate.total — counter (gate_id, result)
5. ao.stream.first_token_ms — histogram

Sorular:
Q1: Bu span ve metrik listesi dogru mu? Eksik/fazla?
Q2: OTEL yuklu degilse graceful degrade nasil olmali? No-op tracer mi, import-time check mi?
Q3: Instrumentation nereye konmali? Ayri modul mu (ao_kernel/telemetry.py), yoksa her moduldeki fonksiyonlara decorator mi?
Q4: Semantic conventions: Gen AI OTEL conventions (gen_ai.*) mi kullanalim, yoksa custom ao.* namespace mi?

Kisa yanit ver. Turkce.

options.title: Core spans only
options.description: llm_call + stream_call + policy_check spanlari, metrikler sonra
